148	- Spec gap: story acceptance says advisory lock keyed on `(user_id, scrape_mode)`; spec §15 keys it on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)`. Functionally equivalent (social_links has partial unique on `(user_id, platform)` constraining the family) — followed spec verbatim and noted in migration header.
149	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
150	  - The `set search_path = ''` + `public.<type>` qualification pattern is the new default for every PL/pgSQL function in this repo — promoted to Codebase Patterns as a single consolidated rule (with the GREATEST gotcha called out explicitly). Save future iterations the cost of rediscovering this through advisor warnings + 42883 errors at trigger fire time.
151	  - When a SECURITY DEFINER function lands in a story, the search_path hardening is not optional follow-up work — bake it into the original migration to avoid the 4-migration sequence we ended up with here.
152	  - Test trigger functions end-to-end via `execute_sql` DO blocks that capture assertions into a temp table — `RAISE NOTICE` doesn't surface through Supabase MCP.
153	  - Reviewer's "false sense of out-of-order protection" framing on `greatest()` is a great pattern: a guard that protects one column but leaves siblings unguarded is worse than no guard, because it implies safety. Watch for this shape in future denormalization triggers.
154	---
155	
156	## 2026-04-17 14:00 — US-009
157	- Applied `us_009_rls_policies` via Supabase MCP: two `stable` SQL helpers (`public.current_user_id()`, `public.current_user_role()` — `set search_path = ''`, `nullif` on the JWT claim strings, schema-qualified enum cast); `alter table ... enable row level security` on all 15 user-data tables (idempotent); full policy set per spec §4.7 block 14 (users/creator_profiles/lister_profiles/social_links/metric_snapshots/listings/listing_versions/listing_conditions/sample_videos/applications/submissions/submission_videos/notifications/push_tokens — lister-read policies thread through the FK chain so a lister only sees creators who applied to their listings; decision policies on applications/submissions scope writes to the listing owner); `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` keeps events service-role only.
158	- Dropped `public.submission_reuse_view` (US-006 handoff TODO: RLS would hide cross-lister reuses) and replaced with SECURITY DEFINER `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` — does its own ownership check via `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, returns NULL to non-owning listers, bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses.
159	- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: Views block now empty; three new entries appear under Functions (`current_user_id: string`, `current_user_role: user_role`, `submission_reuse_count: number` with `{ p_submission_id: string }` args). Note: generator declares `current_user_id.Returns: string` but the routine can return NULL — callers should narrow.
160	- Verified via transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block: seeded 4 users (2 creators, 2 listers), 2 listings (1 active, 1 draft), 1 application, impersonated each via `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims"`. All 11 RLS assertions pass (creator sees own + active listings, not other creators; lister sees own applicants, not cross-lister creators; draft listings hidden from non-owners). All 4 `submission_reuse_count` assertions pass (owning lister sees count; non-owning lister gets NULL).
161	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
162	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` — intentional per spec §4.4 line 1083: service-role only) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
163	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One Medium observation downgraded in-report to a documentation nit (the `nullif` wrapper on `current_user_role` is a silent spec deviation — intentional hardening, policies don't depend on NULL semantics there). Not fixed in this commit to avoid editing an already-applied migration.
164	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
165	  - The helper-routine pattern (`current_user_id()`, `current_user_role()`) is cleaner than inlining `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'` in every policy; promoted to Codebase Patterns.
166	  - The view-to-routine swap resolves US-006's handoff cleanly — any future aggregate that must cross tenants should be a SECURITY DEFINER routine with its own gating, not a view.
167	  - Transactional RLS test pattern (begin + set local role + assertions + rollback) is repeatable; promoted to Codebase Patterns with the gotchas baked in.
168	  - `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — using it outside a DO block raises a parser error.
169	---
170	
171	## 2026-04-17 15:10 — US-010
172	- Applied `us_010_cascade_trigger` (BEFORE UPDATE trigger `trg_bump_listing_version` on `public.listings` + `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` helper) per docs/tech-architecture.md §15 lines 1103-1203. Trigger branches on scalar field diffs (price_cents/currency/max_submissions) + the ephemeral `version_bump_reason` cue for conditions/sample_videos edits, only bumps when new.status='active', snapshots to `listing_versions`, cascades pending `applications` → `cancelled_listing_edit`, emits `notifications` row per affected creator.
173	- Second migration `us_010_fix_array_append` fixes a `text[] || 'literal'` crash that hit at first trigger fire (22P02 "malformed array literal"). Root cause: with `set search_path=''`, Postgres resolves the `||` operator against unknown-type literals as `anyarray || anyarray`. Fix: cast every literal to `::text`. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
174	- End-to-end verified via `execute_sql` transactional block: 18 assertions covering price bump (version+1, current_version_id swapped, application cascaded, notification payload correct), title-only edit (no bump), status=paused edit (no bump), `request_listing_version_bump(lid, 'conditions')` (bump fires, cue consumed to NULL on disk, changed_fields={conditions}, pending apps cascaded), and invalid reason raises. All pass.
175	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417150000_us_010_cascade_trigger.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417150100_us_010_fix_array_append.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (no public schema additions; `app_private` functions not exposed in generated types).
176	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` — intentional per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
177	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. Two notes surfaced as forward-looking concerns, neither medium-or-higher: (a) snapshot's self-referential `current_version_id` field reflects the old version, not the new — spec-faithful (spec's `to_jsonb(new)` at line 1132 runs before the swap); `previous_version_id` column carries the correct linked-list edge, so this is a curio not a bug. (b) §15b threshold triggers were intentionally scoped out of US-010; promoted to Codebase Patterns as a hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055.
178	- Spec gap: §15b threshold triggers (`refresh_listing_thresholds`, `refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump`) are NOT in US-010 — scoped to §15 cascade trigger only per AC. Without them, `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` go stale on conditions-only bumps. Tracked as a Codebase Patterns hard dependency.
179	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
180	  - `text[] || 'literal'` is a silent time bomb in `search_path=''` functions — it parses fine (create-time), fails at first fire. Always cast literals to `::text` when appending to a text array inside hardened PL/pgSQL.
181	  - The `to_jsonb(new)` snapshot inside a BEFORE trigger captures the NEW record mid-mutation. If the trigger later sets `new.current_version_id` (or any other column), the snapshot reflects the pre-set value, not the final written state. Harmless when paired with dedicated columns (like `previous_version_id`) but worth knowing.
182	  - When a story intentionally defers part of a spec section, treat it as a hard dependency and promote it to Codebase Patterns so the next iteration that might ship something load-bearing on the deferred part sees it first. Migration-header comments alone aren't enough — iterations read Codebase Patterns first.
183	---
184	
185	## 2026-04-17 16:35 — US-011
186	- Applied `us_011_cron_jobs` via Supabase MCP: enabled `pg_net` extension and scheduled two pg_cron jobs — `mark-metrics-stale` (hourly `0 * * * *`, verbatim from spec §16: flips `metric_snapshots.status` `fresh→stale` past 24h with `is_latest`) and `cron-refresh-metrics` (daily `0 4 * * *`: POSTs to the eventually-deployed `cron-refresh-metrics` edge function via `net.http_post`, reading URL + service-role key from `vault.decrypted_secrets`). Idempotent re-scheduling guards with `cron.unschedule` existence checks.
187	- Follow-up `us_011_fix_silent_no_secret` rewrites the daily cron's body as a `do $body$ ... $body$` PL/pgSQL block that resolves both vault secrets first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` if either is NULL — fixes a Medium reviewer finding (pg_net silently returns success on `url := NULL`, so a missing vault secret would have logged as a successful run forever instead of surfacing in `cron.job_run_details`). Promoted both the pg_net NULL-trap and the vault-secret pattern to Codebase Patterns.
188	- Verified via `execute_sql` DO block: synthetic 25h-old `fresh`+`is_latest` snapshot flips to `stale` after running the marker body verbatim. Confirmed both jobs registered with `active=true` in `cron.job` (jobid 1 = mark-metrics-stale, jobid 2 = cron-refresh-metrics).
189	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (cron jobs aren't surfaced in the public schema).
190	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
191	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Medium: silent vault-NULL no-op resolved by the follow-up migration). Reviewer also surfaced two spec-gap notes (orphaned non-`is_latest` fresh rows; §16 has 4 unscheduled jobs) — both promoted to Codebase Patterns rather than fixed in this story per one-story-per-iteration.
192	- Spec gap (story vs spec naming): story AC said "metrics-refresh edge function"; spec §5.2 distinguishes the user-facing `metrics-refresh` (US-021) from the internal `cron-refresh-metrics`. Followed spec naming; the cron deliberately points at the internal function (different rate-limit + auth shape).
193	- Spec gap (§16 unscheduled jobs): see Codebase Patterns. `fail-stuck-refreshing` is the most operationally important — should land with the webhook receiver story group (US-016+).
194	- Spec gap (staleness marker orphan rows): see Codebase Patterns. UI unaffected.
195	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
196	  - pg_net's silent-success-on-NULL is the kind of footgun where the obvious code path looks fine but the failure mode is invisible. When a cron body depends on an external lookup (vault, dynamic config, etc.), wrap it in PL/pgSQL with explicit NULL handling — never inline the lookup as a sub-select directly in `net.http_post(url := ...)`.
197	  - `pg_cron` has no upsert; the `do $$ ... if exists ... then perform cron.unschedule(...) ...` pattern is the standard idempotency wrapper. Save it for any future cron migration.
198	  - `create extension pg_net with schema extensions` is accepted but pg_net always installs functions in the `net` schema regardless of WITH SCHEMA. Document this in the migration header so the next reader doesn't go hunting in `extensions.net.http_post`.
199	  - When the reviewer flags a spec-level gap (not a code bug), promote it to Codebase Patterns rather than fixing it in the current story. The one-story-per-iteration rule trumps "while we're here".
200	---
201	
202	## 2026-04-17 17:05 — US-012
203	- Applied `us_012_seed` via Supabase MCP: one DO block seeds fixed-UUID rows — `acme_studio` lister + `lister_profiles`, `seed_creator` creator + `creator_profiles` + tiktok/instagram social_links, one active listing "Promote Acme Headphones" with listing_version v1 (2 pre-conditions — `min_followers` tiktok/instagram, 2 post-conditions — `post_family_friendly` bool + `post_must_mention` contains, 1 sample_video), and three metric_snapshots (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts) that the US-007 BEFORE-INSERT trigger mirrors into creator_profiles.
204	- Verified via `execute_sql`: creator_profiles denorm columns all populated — tiktok_follower_count=42000, tiktok_avg_views_last_10=18500, tiktok_total_likes=850000, tiktok_video_count=120, tiktok_is_verified=false, instagram_follower_count=15000, instagram_avg_views_last_10=6400, instagram_media_count=55, metrics_fetched_at set. All three metric_snapshots rows `is_latest=true` (one per scrape_mode). listing.current_version_id wired to v1 without re-triggering the US-010 cascade (the follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id — no versioned-field diff).
205	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` unchanged (seed is pure DML — no DDL).
206	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
207	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One cosmetic note (`is_verified=true` on the ig_details row is silently discarded by the denorm trigger since `instagram_is_verified` is not a modeled denorm column — stored in snapshot but harmless; not worth a follow-up migration given append-only rules).
208	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
209	  - Fixed UUIDs for seeded rows (`11111111-1111-1111-1111-1111111110XX` pattern) let later stories reference the seed without having to query-by-handle first. Later stories that rely on the seed (US-038 discover feed filter, US-055 edit-campaign cascade verification, US-057 override-eligibility approval) should import these IDs as constants rather than re-querying.
210	  - Safe pattern for seeding an `active` listing without triggering the US-010 cascade: (a) INSERT listing with `status='active'` + `current_version_id=NULL`, (b) INSERT listing_versions v1, (c) UPDATE only `current_version_id` on the listing. The UPDATE fires `trg_bump_listing_version` but `price_cents`/`currency`/`max_submissions` are unchanged and `version_bump_reason` is NULL, so `versioned_changed=false` and no cascade runs. Avoids the alternative of disabling/re-enabling the trigger mid-migration.
211	  - `denorm_metrics` only mirrors columns that exist on `creator_profiles`. Extra columns on the snapshot (e.g. `is_verified` on `ig_details`) are legal but silently ignored — no Instagram verification denorm column exists per spec §4.7. Don't fabricate values for snapshot columns that don't map anywhere.
212	---
213	
214	## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
215	- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
216	- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
217	- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
218	- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
219	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
220	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
221	- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
222	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
223	  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
224	  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
225	  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
226	  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.
227	---
228	
229	## 2026-04-17 13:00 — US-008
230	- Applied `us_008_notifications_events` via Supabase MCP: `public.notifications` (6 cols, inbox + unread partial indexes), `public.push_tokens` (6 cols, unique `expo_token`, ios/android check), `public.events` (bigserial PK audit log with composite `(entity, entity_id, created_at desc)` index). RLS enabled on all three; policies deferred to US-009. `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` enforces app-role append-only per spec §4.4 line 1086.
231	- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: three new tables appear with correct shapes (events.id → `number` from bigserial; notifications.kind → `notification_kind` enum; push_tokens.platform → `string` since the spec uses a CHECK not an enum).
232	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417130000_us_008_notifications_events.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
233	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables + the 12 pre-existing — all deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
234	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One medium observation flagged: append-only enforcement scope for `events` — migration matches the spec literally but service_role retains UPDATE/DELETE grants. Promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-016+ handoff (edge functions writing to events must discipline themselves to INSERT-only; no DB trigger backstop exists).
235	- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.
236	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
237	  - Tables that the spec defines for future-version features (e.g. push_tokens for v1.1) should still be created when they appear in the same §4.7 block as an in-scope table. Partial block creation causes drift that later iterations would have to reconcile.
238	  - `revoke all on <table> from anon, authenticated` + RLS-enabled + zero policies = full deny for app roles. Useful pattern for anything in `public` that must be service-role-only (events-style audit logs, internal job queues).
239	  - When the reviewer flags a concern that the spec itself codifies, surface it as a forward-looking Codebase Patterns handoff rather than a migration-time fix — fixing it in the story would drift from the spec, which has a stronger hard-rule in this repo.
240	---
241

# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.

- Dropped `public.submission_reuse_view` (US-006 handoff TODO: RLS would hide cross-lister reuses) and replaced with SECURITY DEFINER `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` — does its own ownership check via `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, returns NULL to non-owning listers, bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses.
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: Views block now empty; three new entries appear under Functions (`current_user_id: string`, `current_user_role: user_role`, `submission_reuse_count: number` with `{ p_submission_id: string }` args). Note: generator declares `current_user_id.Returns: string` but the routine can return NULL — callers should narrow.
- Verified via transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block: seeded 4 users (2 creators, 2 listers), 2 listings (1 active, 1 draft), 1 application, impersonated each via `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims"`. All 11 RLS assertions pass (creator sees own + active listings, not other creators; lister sees own applicants, not cross-lister creators; draft listings hidden from non-owners). All 4 `submission_reuse_count` assertions pass (owning lister sees count; non-owning lister gets NULL).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` — intentional per spec §4.4 line 1083: service-role only) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One Medium observation downgraded in-report to a documentation nit (the `nullif` wrapper on `current_user_role` is a silent spec deviation — intentional hardening, policies don't depend on NULL semantics there). Not fixed in this commit to avoid editing an already-applied migration.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The helper-routine pattern (`current_user_id()`, `current_user_role()`) is cleaner than inlining `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'` in every policy; promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - The view-to-routine swap resolves US-006's handoff cleanly — any future aggregate that must cross tenants should be a SECURITY DEFINER routine with its own gating, not a view.
  - Transactional RLS test pattern (begin + set local role + assertions + rollback) is repeatable; promoted to Codebase Patterns with the gotchas baked in.
  - `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — using it outside a DO block raises a parser error.
---

## 2026-04-17 15:10 — US-010
- Applied `us_010_cascade_trigger` (BEFORE UPDATE trigger `trg_bump_listing_version` on `public.listings` + `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` helper) per docs/tech-architecture.md §15 lines 1103-1203. Trigger branches on scalar field diffs (price_cents/currency/max_submissions) + the ephemeral `version_bump_reason` cue for conditions/sample_videos edits, only bumps when new.status='active', snapshots to `listing_versions`, cascades pending `applications` → `cancelled_listing_edit`, emits `notifications` row per affected creator.
- Second migration `us_010_fix_array_append` fixes a `text[] || 'literal'` crash that hit at first trigger fire (22P02 "malformed array literal"). Root cause: with `set search_path=''`, Postgres resolves the `||` operator against unknown-type literals as `anyarray || anyarray`. Fix: cast every literal to `::text`. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
- End-to-end verified via `execute_sql` transactional block: 18 assertions covering price bump (version+1, current_version_id swapped, application cascaded, notification payload correct), title-only edit (no bump), status=paused edit (no bump), `request_listing_version_bump(lid, 'conditions')` (bump fires, cue consumed to NULL on disk, changed_fields={conditions}, pending apps cascaded), and invalid reason raises. All pass.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417150000_us_010_cascade_trigger.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417150100_us_010_fix_array_append.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (no public schema additions; `app_private` functions not exposed in generated types).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` — intentional per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. Two notes surfaced as forward-looking concerns, neither medium-or-higher: (a) snapshot's self-referential `current_version_id` field reflects the old version, not the new — spec-faithful (spec's `to_jsonb(new)` at line 1132 runs before the swap); `previous_version_id` column carries the correct linked-list edge, so this is a curio not a bug. (b) §15b threshold triggers were intentionally scoped out of US-010; promoted to Codebase Patterns as a hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055.
- Spec gap: §15b threshold triggers (`refresh_listing_thresholds`, `refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump`) are NOT in US-010 — scoped to §15 cascade trigger only per AC. Without them, `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` go stale on conditions-only bumps. Tracked as a Codebase Patterns hard dependency.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `text[] || 'literal'` is a silent time bomb in `search_path=''` functions — it parses fine (create-time), fails at first fire. Always cast literals to `::text` when appending to a text array inside hardened PL/pgSQL.
  - The `to_jsonb(new)` snapshot inside a BEFORE trigger captures the NEW record mid-mutation. If the trigger later sets `new.current_version_id` (or any other column), the snapshot reflects the pre-set value, not the final written state. Harmless when paired with dedicated columns (like `previous_version_id`) but worth knowing.
  - When a story intentionally defers part of a spec section, treat it as a hard dependency and promote it to Codebase Patterns so the next iteration that might ship something load-bearing on the deferred part sees it first. Migration-header comments alone aren't enough — iterations read Codebase Patterns first.
---

## 2026-04-17 16:35 — US-011
- Applied `us_011_cron_jobs` via Supabase MCP: enabled `pg_net` extension and scheduled two pg_cron jobs — `mark-metrics-stale` (hourly `0 * * * *`, verbatim from spec §16: flips `metric_snapshots.status` `fresh→stale` past 24h with `is_latest`) and `cron-refresh-metrics` (daily `0 4 * * *`: POSTs to the eventually-deployed `cron-refresh-metrics` edge function via `net.http_post`, reading URL + service-role key from `vault.decrypted_secrets`). Idempotent re-scheduling guards with `cron.unschedule` existence checks.
- Follow-up `us_011_fix_silent_no_secret` rewrites the daily cron's body as a `do $body$ ... $body$` PL/pgSQL block that resolves both vault secrets first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` if either is NULL — fixes a Medium reviewer finding (pg_net silently returns success on `url := NULL`, so a missing vault secret would have logged as a successful run forever instead of surfacing in `cron.job_run_details`). Promoted both the pg_net NULL-trap and the vault-secret pattern to Codebase Patterns.
- Verified via `execute_sql` DO block: synthetic 25h-old `fresh`+`is_latest` snapshot flips to `stale` after running the marker body verbatim. Confirmed both jobs registered with `active=true` in `cron.job` (jobid 1 = mark-metrics-stale, jobid 2 = cron-refresh-metrics).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (cron jobs aren't surfaced in the public schema).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Medium: silent vault-NULL no-op resolved by the follow-up migration). Reviewer also surfaced two spec-gap notes (orphaned non-`is_latest` fresh rows; §16 has 4 unscheduled jobs) — both promoted to Codebase Patterns rather than fixed in this story per one-story-per-iteration.
- Spec gap (story vs spec naming): story AC said "metrics-refresh edge function"; spec §5.2 distinguishes the user-facing `metrics-refresh` (US-021) from the internal `cron-refresh-metrics`. Followed spec naming; the cron deliberately points at the internal function (different rate-limit + auth shape).
- Spec gap (§16 unscheduled jobs): see Codebase Patterns. `fail-stuck-refreshing` is the most operationally important — should land with the webhook receiver story group (US-016+).
- Spec gap (staleness marker orphan rows): see Codebase Patterns. UI unaffected.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - pg_net's silent-success-on-NULL is the kind of footgun where the obvious code path looks fine but the failure mode is invisible. When a cron body depends on an external lookup (vault, dynamic config, etc.), wrap it in PL/pgSQL with explicit NULL handling — never inline the lookup as a sub-select directly in `net.http_post(url := ...)`.
  - `pg_cron` has no upsert; the `do $$ ... if exists ... then perform cron.unschedule(...) ...` pattern is the standard idempotency wrapper. Save it for any future cron migration.
  - `create extension pg_net with schema extensions` is accepted but pg_net always installs functions in the `net` schema regardless of WITH SCHEMA. Document this in the migration header so the next reader doesn't go hunting in `extensions.net.http_post`.
  - When the reviewer flags a spec-level gap (not a code bug), promote it to Codebase Patterns rather than fixing it in the current story. The one-story-per-iteration rule trumps "while we're here".
---

## 2026-04-17 17:05 — US-012
- Applied `us_012_seed` via Supabase MCP: one DO block seeds fixed-UUID rows — `acme_studio` lister + `lister_profiles`, `seed_creator` creator + `creator_profiles` + tiktok/instagram social_links, one active listing "Promote Acme Headphones" with listing_version v1 (2 pre-conditions — `min_followers` tiktok/instagram, 2 post-conditions — `post_family_friendly` bool + `post_must_mention` contains, 1 sample_video), and three metric_snapshots (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts) that the US-007 BEFORE-INSERT trigger mirrors into creator_profiles.
- Verified via `execute_sql`: creator_profiles denorm columns all populated — tiktok_follower_count=42000, tiktok_avg_views_last_10=18500, tiktok_total_likes=850000, tiktok_video_count=120, tiktok_is_verified=false, instagram_follower_count=15000, instagram_avg_views_last_10=6400, instagram_media_count=55, metrics_fetched_at set. All three metric_snapshots rows `is_latest=true` (one per scrape_mode). listing.current_version_id wired to v1 without re-triggering the US-010 cascade (the follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id — no versioned-field diff).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` unchanged (seed is pure DML — no DDL).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One cosmetic note (`is_verified=true` on the ig_details row is silently discarded by the denorm trigger since `instagram_is_verified` is not a modeled denorm column — stored in snapshot but harmless; not worth a follow-up migration given append-only rules).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Fixed UUIDs for seeded rows (`11111111-1111-1111-1111-1111111110XX` pattern) let later stories reference the seed without having to query-by-handle first. Later stories that rely on the seed (US-038 discover feed filter, US-055 edit-campaign cascade verification, US-057 override-eligibility approval) should import these IDs as constants rather than re-querying.
  - Safe pattern for seeding an `active` listing without triggering the US-010 cascade: (a) INSERT listing with `status='active'` + `current_version_id=NULL`, (b) INSERT listing_versions v1, (c) UPDATE only `current_version_id` on the listing. The UPDATE fires `trg_bump_listing_version` but `price_cents`/`currency`/`max_submissions` are unchanged and `version_bump_reason` is NULL, so `versioned_changed=false` and no cascade runs. Avoids the alternative of disabling/re-enabling the trigger mid-migration.
  - `denorm_metrics` only mirrors columns that exist on `creator_profiles`. Extra columns on the snapshot (e.g. `is_verified` on `ig_details`) are legal but silently ignored — no Instagram verification denorm column exists per spec §4.7. Don't fabricate values for snapshot columns that don't map anywhere.
---

## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.
---

## 2026-04-17 13:00 — US-008
- Applied `us_008_notifications_events` via Supabase MCP: `public.notifications` (6 cols, inbox + unread partial indexes), `public.push_tokens` (6 cols, unique `expo_token`, ios/android check), `public.events` (bigserial PK audit log with composite `(entity, entity_id, created_at desc)` index). RLS enabled on all three; policies deferred to US-009. `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` enforces app-role append-only per spec §4.4 line 1086.
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: three new tables appear with correct shapes (events.id → `number` from bigserial; notifications.kind → `notification_kind` enum; push_tokens.platform → `string` since the spec uses a CHECK not an enum).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417130000_us_008_notifications_events.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables + the 12 pre-existing — all deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One medium observation flagged: append-only enforcement scope for `events` — migration matches the spec literally but service_role retains UPDATE/DELETE grants. Promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-016+ handoff (edge functions writing to events must discipline themselves to INSERT-only; no DB trigger backstop exists).
- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Tables that the spec defines for future-version features (e.g. push_tokens for v1.1) should still be created when they appear in the same §4.7 block as an in-scope table. Partial block creation causes drift that later iterations would have to reconcile.
  - `revoke all on <table> from anon, authenticated` + RLS-enabled + zero policies = full deny for app roles. Useful pattern for anything in `public` that must be service-role-only (events-style audit logs, internal job queues).
  - When the reviewer flags a concern that the spec itself codifies, surface it as a forward-looking Codebase Patterns handoff rather than a migration-time fix — fixing it in the story would drift from the spec, which has a stronger hard-rule in this repo.
---

content.content:
# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the tight summary:

---

## US-014 Research Summary

**1. DATABASE SCHEMA (§4.7 + §4.4)**

**`public.users` (required columns on INSERT):**
- `id` (uuid, auto-generated via `gen_random_uuid()`)
- `role` (user_role NOT NULL, enum: "creator"|"lister") — **required**
- `username` (citext NOT NULL UNIQUE) — **required**
- `email` (citext UNIQUE nullable) — **required for lister** (optional for creator)
- `created_at` (timestamptz default now())
- `updated_at` (timestamptz default now())
- `deleted_at` (timestamptz nullable)

**`public.lister_profiles` (required columns on INSERT):**
- `user_id` (uuid PRIMARY KEY, FK to users, ON DELETE CASCADE) — **required**
- `org_name` (text NOT NULL) — **required**
- `website_url` (text nullable, optional)
- `logo_path` (text nullable, optional)
- `description` (text nullable, optional)
- `created_at` (timestamptz default now())
- `updated_at` (timestamptz default now())

**2. `auth-signup-lister` SPEC (§5.1)**

**Request:** `POST /auth/signup-lister`
```ts
{ username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
```

**Response (200):**
```ts
{ user_id: string; jwt: string; }
```

**Status codes:**
- `200 OK` on success
- `409 USERNAME_TAKEN` if username already exists
- `409 EMAIL_TAKEN` if email already exists  
- `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` if ≥5 signup attempts from same IP within 60 minutes

**Transaction contract:** Both `users` and `lister_profiles` rows inserted in a single Postgres transaction. Idempotent on username (checked before any write).

**Pre-insert validation:**
- Username uniqueness: reject with 409 if exists (case-insensitive via citext)
- Email uniqueness: reject with 409 if exists (case-insensitive)
- Per-IP rate limit via `signup_attempts` table: hash client IP with salt, allow max 5 attempts/60min; return `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` with `retry_after_sec`

**No password/credential model:** v1 is JWT-only. Users sign up with username+email+org_name; edge function generates `session_id` (UUID), signs JWT with `signJwt()`, returns token + user_id.

**3. SESSION & JWT HANDLING (§4.4 + §5.1 signout)**

**JWT payload (§4.4):**
```json
{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
```

**Session management:**
- `session_id` is server-generated (UUID v4) at signup, **not client-provided**
- No `sessions` table; revocation tracked in `public.revoked_sessions` (§15c)
- `POST /auth/signout` inserts revocation row (idempotent by session_id)
- Edge functions use `signJwt()` from `_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256, HMAC-SHA256, secret from env var `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`)
- RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'` (JWT from custom signer, not Supabase Auth)

**4. SIGNUP ATTEMPTS & REVOKED SESSIONS TABLES**

Both exist per migrations:
- `public.signup_attempts` (created: US-012 seed migration) — `(id, ip_hash, user_agent, role_attempted, success, created_at)`, indexed on `(ip_hash, created_at DESC)`, pruned >7d by cron
- `public.revoked_sessions` (created: §15c in migration) — `(session_id UUID PK, user_id, revoked_at, reason, expires_at)`, cached in-memory with LISTEN/NOTIFY invalidation, 5-min TTL

No separate signup-attempts logging for lister (shared IP throttle with creator).

**5. Insert Types from `src/types/supabase.ts`**

**`users.Insert`:**
```ts
{ role: "lister"; username: string; email?: string; id?: string; created_at?: string; updated_at?: string; deleted_at?: string; }
```

**`lister_profiles.Insert`:**
```ts
{ user_id: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string | null; logo_path?: string | null; description?: string | null; created_at?: string; updated_at?: string; }
```

**6. SHARED HELPERS in `_shared/`**

- `jwt.ts` — `signJwt(input, options?) → Promise<string>`, `verifyJwt(token) → Promise<JwtClaims>`
- `cors.ts` — CORS handling

**7. MIGRATIONS EXIST**

All schema migrations from US-003 through US-012 exist; US-014 will use the already-migrated tables (no new migrations needed for schema).

**8. SCRIPTS STRUCTURE**

Only `scripts/ralph/` exists (test/demo data for earlier stories); no generic `scripts/test/` folder. `test/` would be in root if present. Pattern: edge functions are tested via curl in the docs, not via a scripts folder.

**No password hashing, no email verification, no KYC.** Email is self-declared; username uniqueness and email uniqueness are the only pre-insert validations besides the per-IP throttle.

content.text:
Confirmed: `signup_attempts` and `revoked_sessions` don't exist yet — Codebase Patterns §55 notes they belong to a future story group. US-014's AC doesn't require throttling, so I'll scope minimally and flag the deferred throttle as a spec gap.

Let me read the exact spec for the signup-lister contract and confirm schemas.

content.content:
1560	  display_name?: string;
1561	  country?: string;
1562	}
1563	interface Res {
1564	  user_id: string;
1565	  jwt: string;
1566	  social_links: { id: string; platform: Platform; handle: string; status: 'linked' }[];
1567	  initial_scrape_queued: boolean;
1568	}
1569	```
1570	**Transaction boundary:** the identity rows (`users`, `creator_profiles`, `social_links`, and pre-created `refreshing` `metric_snapshots` rows) commit in a single Postgres transaction. Apify runs are dispatched **after** the transaction commits — a dispatch failure does NOT roll back the account. The creator gets a working account, the `social_links` row, and a `refreshing` snapshot; the UI displays "Couldn't fetch metrics — we'll retry automatically" and the daily cron picks the link up within 24h. This is intentional: Apify outages shouldn't block signup.
1571	
1572	**Per-run outcomes after the commit** (handled inline in the edge function before returning):
1573	- If `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` returns with `status: 'SUCCEEDED'` and items → parse + UPDATE snapshot to `fresh` inline. Counts toward the response's `initial_scrape_queued = false` flag per-platform.
1574	- If it returns with `status: 'RUNNING'` (inside-wait timeout) → webhook completes later. `initial_scrape_queued = true`.
1575	- If it throws (rate limit, 4xx/5xx from Apify) → UPDATE snapshot to `failed` with `error_message`, increment `social_links.fail_count`, return normally. The UI chips the failed mode as "Couldn't fetch" with a retry button.
1576	
1577	Signup is idempotent on `(username)` — a duplicate call returns `409 USERNAME_TAKEN` before any Apify dispatch.
1578	
1579	**Per-IP throttle:** the edge function computes `sha256(ip + SIGNUP_SALT)` and inserts a `signup_attempts` row. If ≥5 attempts from the same `ip_hash` occurred in the prior 60 minutes (regardless of success/failure), return `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` with `retry_after_sec`. Salt rotates quarterly.
1580	
1581	Errors: `400 MISSING_HANDLE`, `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1582	
1583	**`POST /auth/signup-lister`**
1584	```ts
1585	interface Req { username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
1586	interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
1587	```
1588	Same per-IP throttle as `signup-creator` (shared `signup_attempts` table).
1589	Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1590	
1591	**`POST /auth/signout`**
1592	```ts
1593	interface Req { session_id: string; }
1594	interface Res { ok: true; }
1595	```
1596	Inserts a row into `revoked_sessions` with `expires_at = <jwt exp claim>`. Edge middleware caches the revocation set in-memory (5-min TTL; `LISTEN revoked_sessions_invalidate` pushes inserts live, with force-reload on reconnect — see §15c comments). JWTs with `session_id` in the set are rejected with `401`. Max stale-revocation window = 5 min (TTL) worst-case after a container cold-start that misses a NOTIFY during the one-request reload gap; typical window is zero because NOTIFY is synchronous.
1597	
1598	### 5.2 Social Handles & Metrics
1599	
1600	**`POST /social/add-handle`** — creator links an additional platform
1601	```ts
1602	interface Req { platform: Platform; handle: string; }
1603	interface Res { social_link_id: string; scrape_run_id: string; }
1604	```
1605	Side effects: insert `social_links` + kick initial Apify run (async, webhook-completed).
1606	Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `409 PLATFORM_ALREADY_LINKED`.
1607	
1608	**`POST /social/update-handle`** — creator changed their username on the platform
1609	```ts
1610	interface Req { social_link_id: string; new_handle: string; }
1611	interface Res { ok: true; scrape_run_id: string; }
1612	```
1613	Side effects: update handle + trigger fresh scrape. Old snapshots retained.
1614	Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`.
1615	
1616	**`POST /social/unlink`**
1617	```ts
1618	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1619	interface Res { ok: true; }
1620	```
1621	Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` (creator must keep ≥1 linked platform).
1622	
1623	**`POST /metrics/refresh`** — creator-triggered, rate-limited
1624	```ts
1625	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1626	interface Res {
1627	  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
1628	  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
1629	  retry_after_sec?: number;
1630	}
1631	```
1632	Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1633	
1634	| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
1635	|---|---|---|
1636	| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
1637	| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |
1638	
1639	Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
1640	
1641	**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
1642	- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
1643	- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
1644	- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
1645	- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
1646	  ```sql
1647	  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
1648	  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
1649	  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
1650	  -- Apify runs are queued.
1651	  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
1652	  ```
1653	  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.
1654	
1655	### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver
1656

1667	  social_link_id: string;
1668	  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
1669	  run_id: string;
